description: A message carries two passing DKIM signatures: one aligned with From (header.d=example.com) and one for attacker-controlled evil.test. A single divergent signing domain is enough to flag, so dkimDomains is [example.com, evil.test], dkimDomainMatchesFromDomain is false, and one low-severity dkim.domainMismatch signal is emitted carrying only the mismatching subset (evil.test). Multiple signatures are routine for legitimate mail (author domain plus forwarder/list), which is why the severity stays low and the caller is expected to note that one signature did align.

authentication-results: mx.example.net; dmarc=pass header.from=example.com; spf=pass smtp.mailfrom=example.com; dkim=pass header.d=example.com; dkim=pass header.d=evil.test

authenticationResults.raw: mx.example.net; dmarc=pass header.from=example.com; spf=pass smtp.mailfrom=example.com; dkim=pass header.d=example.com; dkim=pass header.d=evil.test

signals.message: DKIM header.d signing domain differs from the From domain.
